name: Split Pea & Lamb Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 package Split peas, green or yellow
1/2 lb Boned lamb shoulder or neck
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort peas and discard any debris. Rinse the peas and drain them; set aside.
2. In a 5-6 quart pan over high heat, combine celery, onion, lamb, garlic, bay leaf, and 1/2 cup water. Cover and simmer rapidly for 10 minutes. Uncover and turn the heat to high. Stir often until browned bits form in the pan. Deglaze by adding 1/3 cup water and stirring to scrape up the browned bits. Continue stirring until the liquid evaporates and new browned bits form. Repeat deglazing several more times until the vegetables are a rich brown, about 30 minutes total.
3. To the pan, add split peas and 7 cups chicken broth; bring to a boil over high heat. Cover and simmer until the peas mash easily, about 1 hour.
4. Discard the bay leaf. Transfer 3 cups soup (without meat) to a blender or food processor and whirl until smoothly pureed. Return to the pan. For a thinner soup, add more broth. Stir on high heat until hot.
